title: Vision Supervisors Overview
description: A guided tour of the Vision system: foundational sources, hidden PING/PONG, and the supervisor-style behavior addons attached to Screen, Clipboard, Webcam, and Heart Rate.

steps.title: Welcome
steps.body: This tutorial explains the newer Vision architecture. The pattern is: foundational source first, then one or more child behavior addons that react in persona.

steps.title: Open the Vision Host Tab
steps.body: The Vision system lives inside Host -> Vision. This is where hidden sensory input, hidden PING/PONG, and source-specific tabs come together.

steps.title: Included Sources
steps.body: This control decides which sensory sources are active enough to get their own Vision sub-tabs. If a source tab is missing later in this tutorial, check here first.

steps.title: Hidden PING/PONG
steps.body: This enables the background hidden loop. When NC is idle, it can observe selected sensory sources, retain meaningful events, and use those events as ambient internal context later.

steps.title: Automatic Speech
steps.body: This controls whether hidden PONG results are allowed to proactively speak. It affects background interruptions, not ordinary visible user turns.

steps.title: Automatic Visual Replies
steps.body: This is the master auto-image switch. When it is off, NC should not spontaneously generate images from hidden PONG visual requests or from assistant [visualize: ...] tags.

steps.title: Screen as a Source
steps.body: The Screen tab is for ambient desktop context. Its child Supervisor addon is best for reacting to what the user is doing on-screen, not to deliberate uploads.

steps.title: Clipboard as a Source
steps.body: Clipboard is more deliberate than Screen. It watches newly copied images, so its Supervisor tends to be more confident and more directly tied to the user's chosen reference image.

steps.title: Webcam as a Source
steps.body: Webcam is for posture, gaze, expression, props, and visible energy. Its Supervisor behaves like the others, but its evidence comes from the user's current appearance instead of the desktop.

steps.title: Heart Rate as a Source
steps.body: Heart Rate uses the same supervisor pattern, but the trigger field is named Signal Trigger instead of Visual Trigger. It reacts to BPM states rather than images.

steps.title: The Shared Pattern
steps.body: Across all of these, the mental model is now consistent: source tab, child behavior tab, persona, trigger, action, and optional Advanced controls. That consistency is the real feature.
